Document:
# Diona
[bold]Dionae[/bold] are an [bold][color=#4df]Oxygen-breathing[/color][/bold], [bold][color=#f80]Sap-blooded[/color][/bold] species; outwardly resembling animate trees in a humanoid shape.
Dionae are comprised of [bold]3 Nymphs[/bold] which house their 3 organs, together forming a [bold]Gestalt[/bold] with interoperative brains, lungs, and stomach.
Due to their bodies mostly being comprised of wood, Dionae have a [bold]higher body mass[/bold] than most species; the most immediate consequences being [bold]having an easier time dragging objects[/bold] and [bold]difficulty being dragged by others[/bold].
Dionae might [bold][color=#ff0]catch on fire[/color][/bold] if they take significant [bold][color=#f44]Heat[/color] damage[/bold]!
Dionae [bold]get hungry and thirsty 2 times slower[/bold] than most other species.
They take less [bold][color=#4df]Asphyxiation damage[/color][/bold] when suffocating and recover from it faster.
Dionae [bold]benefit from fertilizers[/bold] and other botanical chemicals, but [bold][color=#ff0]get hurt by herbicides[/color][/bold]!
Due to the shape of their legs, Dionae can't wear shoes. But, they can [bold]root[/bold] into the ground at will - keeping them from slipping, keeping them on the ground in absence of gravity, and absorbing any puddles they walk over.
When the Gestalt dies, a Diona may [bold]split[/bold]! Scattering their Nymphs as individual organisms, with the Nymph formerly housing the brain retaining the ability to speak. After [bold]a long wait[/bold], split Nymphs may [bold]regrow[/bold] into a fully fledged Diona, producing new Nymphs with the needed organs.
Dionae have the baseline body temperature of [bold][color=yellow]37°C[/color][/bold] (~[color=yellow]310°K[/color]). They experience the effects of [bold]hypothermia at [color=#4df]-13°C[/color][/bold] and [bold]hyperthermia at [color=#f44]52°C[/color][/bold]. (~[color=#4df]260°K[/color] & [color=#f44]325°K[/color])
Dionae are [bold]omnivorous[/bold], meaning they can safely digest [bold]meat and plant based foods[/bold], but they can't stomach [bold][color=#f44]raw meat or blood[/color][/bold].
Their bodies can process [bold]6 reagents[/bold] at a time.
They slowly [bold]passively regenerate[/bold] all types of [bold][color=yellow]Brute[/color]damage [/bold], and individually [bold][color=#f44]Heat[/color]damage [/bold].
Dionae names typically follow a "[bold]the Noun of Adjective[/bold]" pattern, with nouns generally associated with plants or natural locations.
